world.title: Tokyo Dome City worker dies after being trapped in ride mechanism
world.description: Tokyo Dome City Corporation says an employee died after becoming trapped in the mechanism of the "Flying Balloon" amusement ride during routine maintenance on Tuesday.

world.title: Does the White House have an alcohol problem?
world.description: According to a recent, explosive report in The Atlantic, FBI director Kash Patel likes to drink - sometimes to excess. The FBI Director’s alleged exploits - which he denies, and is suing the magazine over - raise fresh questions about the conduct of people Donald Trump has surrounded himself with at the very top of his administration.

world.title: US potentially facing 'near-term risk' of running out of key missiles
world.description: According to an analysis conducted by the Center for Strategic and International Studies, it may take three to five years to replenish US missile stockpiles to pre-war levels.

world.title: Two CIA officers killed in Mexico
world.description: US agents died in Chihuahua during an anti-drug trafficking operation

world.title: US extends Iran ceasefire for peace talks
world.description: US President Trump indefinitely extends ceasefire with Iran to facilitate ongoing peace talks mediated by Pakistan. Read more at straitstimes.com.

world.title: 'Trust and confidence reposed': Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif thanks Donald Trump for ceasefire extension
world.description: Pakistan News: Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Wednesday thanked US President Donald Trump for extending the ceasefire in the ongoing Iran conflict, expres.

world.title: Airlines demand UK relax noise rules and cut flight tax as fuel shortage looms
world.description: Ministers also asked to alter compensation rights and suspend emissions trading scheme amid Middle East war

world.title: Libya fueled war in Sudan with Colombian mercenaries, UN finds
world.description: United Nations experts have found that paramilitaries fighting against the Sudanese military received support from an armed group in Libya, adding more chaos to the devastating war in Sudan.

business.title: Hong Kong stocks retreat as war uncertainty lingers after ceasefire extension
business.description: Investors need more conviction about material progress on a ceasefire and the normalisation of oil flow, investor says.

business.title: Globe and Grab utilize real-world insights to strengthen network optimization
business.description: Globe has partnered with Grab and its driver-partners to capture real‑world data on connectivity strength and consistency across Metro Manila.

business.title: Taiwan's Smart Tolling Technology Goes Global as Thailand Launches AI-Powered M81 Motorway System
business.description: TAIPEI, April 22, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Sightings of electronic toll collection (ETC) gantries resembling those used on Taiwan's freeways have recently drawn attention on social media along the Bangkok-Kanchanaburi highway. Far Eastern Electronic Toll Collection Co., Ltd. (FETC) confirmed that the system is part of Thailand's newly launched M-Flow multi-lane free-flow tolling system on the Intercity Motorway No. 81 Bang Yai - Kanchanaburi Route (M81).

business.title: Strikes on petrochemical hubs leave Iran short of plastics
business.description: Iran is facing severe shortages of key petrochemical products after recent strikes on its main production hubs, according to two informed sources inside the country.

business.title: Millions of renters hit by unlawful data collection
business.description: Australia’s privacy regulator has found that one of the country’s most widely used rental application platforms collected far more personal information than it needed and used manipulative design tactics to pressure tenants into handing it over.

business.title: Millions of renters hit by unlawful data collection
business.description: Australia’s privacy regulator has found that one of the country’s most widely used rental application platforms collected far more personal information than it needed and used manipulative design tactics to pressure tenants into handing it over.

business.title: US Stock Futures Rise, Dollar Falls on Iran Truce: Markets Wrap
business.description: (Bloomberg) -- US equity-index futures climbed and the dollar edged lower after President Donald Trump said he would extend the ceasefire with Iran, spurring cautious optimism among investors.

business.title: Thailand cuts diesel prices but remains mid-range in ASEAN
business.description: Thailand’s major fuel retailers have cut diesel prices by THB1.20 per litre, offering some relief to motorists as the country continues to grapple with energy price pressures linked to the Middle East crisis.

science.title: Epigenome proteins shape dynamic gene expression beyond simple on-off
science.description: A new study finds the proteins responsible for controlling which genes are expressed in a genome do more than simply turn a gene on or off.

science.title: Stem cell model recreates early human embryo with yolk sac
science.description: For the first time, a stem cell model has produced a structure resembling an early human embryo with a yolk-sac-like structure, from a single starting stem cell population and without direct genetic manipulation.

science.title: Chinese engineers plan to study building greenhouse on lunar surface
science.description: A Chinese lunar exploration team plans to conduct research on a lunar surface greenhouse, according to Wang Qiong, a senior space engineer with the China National Space Administration (CNSA), during a press conference in Beijing on Tuesday.

science.title: Curiosity Explores Sand, Seeks New Drill Target
science.description:

science.title: How does imagination really work in the brain? New theory upends what we knew
science.description: There’s a lot going on in your brain all the time. To power your imagination, the neurons need some silence.

science.title: A Powerful Supercomputer Found A Flaw In A Decades-Old Theory About Sun-Like Stars
science.description: All stars slow their rotation when they age, but new research shows that contrary to previous research, the poles slow down in tandem with the equator.

science.title: Scientists develop MIND approach to predict and reshape microbiomes
science.description: University of California San Diego researchers have developed a new tool for understanding and modifying any microbiome, including the human microbiome.

science.title: New Findings Shed Light on Bronze Age Life in the Caucasus 4,500 Years Ago
science.description: New study reveals the Bronze Age Caucasus diet included wine, dairy, and imported pine resin, uncovered through ancient pottery.

ai.title: Reese Witherspoon responds to criticism over her stance on AI: ‘No one is paying me to talk about this’
ai.description: Reese Witherspoon has responded to backlash over comments on AI, also addressing speculation about whether she was being paid to promote the technology. | Hollywood

ai.title: Trump says Anthropic is 'shaping up,' open to deal with Pentagon
ai.description: After months of tension, the Trump administration’s tone signals a thaw with Anthropic, as legal battles and national security concerns persist. Whether this leads to renewed Pentagon ties or stricter guardrails on military AI use could shape how Washington balances innovation, safety, and control in the escalating global AI race.

ai.title: SpaceX's Galactic IPO: A New Frontier in Business Valuation
ai.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX makes headlines with plans for the largest IPO ever. The company announced a potential $60 billion acquisition of AI startup Cursor and a June market IPO targeting $75 billion capital raising. Despite facing substantial losses, Musk maintains control, driving innovation and investment interest.

ai.title: Meta to track employees' mouse movements, keystrokes to train AI models
ai.description: The initiative, part of Meta's push to build autonomous “AI agents” for workplace tasks, comes alongside workforce restructuring and has sparked concerns among experts over heightened white-collar surveillance and data privacy.

ai.title: This week at SpaceX: AI bets, losses and push for control as mega IPO looms
ai.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX saw a slew of developments this week as it barrels toward what could be the largest initial public offering in history.

ai.title: Feud between AI power startup Fermi and fired CEO and top shareholder heats up over proposed sale
ai.description: The former CEO wants to sell the company to make money for shareholders rather than see Fermi's plans to fruition.

ai.title: Apple's Veteran Engineer John Ternus Ascends to CEO Amid AI Race Uncertainties
ai.description: John Ternus, who has been with Apple for 25 years, is set to take over as CEO from Tim Cook. With a rich hardware engineering background, Ternus faces the challenge of steering Apple in the competitive AI landscape, while managing supply chain dynamics and maintaining strategic partnerships globally.
